title: GetResponse vs Mailgun (2025): Which Is Better?
meta_description: GetResponse vs Mailgun: compare pricing, features, and real use cases to find the right email tool.
intro: Choosing between GetResponse and Mailgun comes down to your specific use case. GetResponse is built for online course creators and webinar hosts, while Mailgun targets developers sending transactional and bulk email via API.

comparison_table.tool_a: Online Course Creators And Webinar Hosts
comparison_table.tool_b: Developers Sending Transactional And Bulk Email Via Api

choose_a_if: You are primarily focused on online course creators and webinar hosts
choose_b_if: You are primarily focused on developers sending transactional and bulk email via API

faq.a: It depends on your use case. GetResponse wins for online course creators and webinar hosts, while Mailgun is stronger for developers sending transactional and bulk email via API.

faq.q: Which is cheaper, GetResponse or Mailgun?
faq.a: Mailgun starts at a lower price point. Pricing scales with subscriber count so total cost depends on your list size.

faq.q: Can I switch from Mailgun to GetResponse?
faq.a: Yes, switching is possible. Most platforms support CSV import/export. Plan time to recreate automations and templates.
